prompt:
Repair `bin/incidentcheck`, the bounded verifier used after an incident mitigation. Its interface is `bash bin/incidentcheck --interval SECONDS --timeout SECONDS --successes COUNT --evidence FILE [--sleep-command PATH] -- HEALTH_COMMAND [ARG ...]`. All numeric values are decimal integers: interval and successes must be positive, while timeout may be zero. The health command is a local, finite shim; exit 0 is healthy and any other exit is unhealthy. The optional sleep adapter is a local executable receiving exactly one delay argument and exists so interval behavior can be tested without wall-clock sleeps.

Probe immediately at elapsed second 0. After a nonterminal observation, wait by the configured interval, capped to the remaining timeout, and probe at the timeout boundary. Recovery requires COUNT consecutive healthy observations; an unhealthy observation resets the streak. Never declare recovery from one isolated green sample when COUNT is greater than one. The loop must always be bounded by its logical timeout, including when a sleep fixture returns immediately. Exit 0 only for verified recovery, 1 when the timeout is reached without the required streak, 64 for invalid invocation, 70 if the sleep adapter fails, and 73 if the evidence file cannot be created.

Preserve the existing tab-separated evidence format and write every probe plus one final result record to FILE. Health command output remains suppressed. The checker must remain offline, deterministic under the supplied shims, and free of background polling. Run `bash tests/test_incidentcheck.sh`; the test is protected and must not be modified.
